mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive equity valuations over a quarter as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction term shows negative out-of-sample IC over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the mechanism reverses direction during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.5%, markets price in imminent aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of cases meeting the threshold, or the inflation level shows no predictive IC over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting sustained consumer demand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The divergent movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC stays below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a demand shock regime signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances following the INDPRO threshold breach, or the signal loses significance outside of identified demand shock periods.
